Strong expertise in building enterprise-grade RAG systems with optimized retrieval strategies, latency-aware design, and cost-efficient LLM usage. Experienced in implementing model lifecycle governance, experiment tracking, A/B testing strategies, and production monitoring. Adept at translating business requirements into scalable AI solutions that improve operational efficiency, reduce costs, and enhance customer experience.

#### Domain Architect - XOps, Enterprise Analytics, and Machine Learning (Current)
...
Ever since moving into the Data Science/Analytics/MLOps space my work has been reinvigorated with a passion for what I'm doing. I've found my fit in this domain and I've been brushing off my theoretical mathematics background, refreshing myself on all the analytics training I've received, and diving headlong into every Data Science, Analytics, and MLOps topic I can find quality content on. I'm more excited than ever to be taking on a role in which I get to combine my understanding of the data science lifecycle and my years of experience architecting reproducible data pipelines, repeatable development processes, rigorous testing standards, reliable and scalable services, and robust orchestration throughout using an industry leading tech stack. Show less
...
#### Expert Data Scientist
...
#### Senior Data Scientist
...
I've spent my career focusing on using empirical evidence to lead me to the best solution for my customers. My passion is serving others and creating workflows that allow people to focus on the creative and most valuable part of their expertise, rather than being stuck carrying out manual tasks or trying to do things that are easier for computers than they are for us. I've missed working with Mathematics and have now been given the opportunity to use my accumulated business knowledge, software development understanding, and passion for positive customer experience to create world class machine learning models through data science. Our goal is to provide solutions to problems we didn't even know we had, to lead the organization in following the data into fact based decision making, and to free our business users to innovate by automating tedious analysis and comparison tasks. I continue to be blessed to get to work with an incredibly diverse and talented team under the leadership of a truly people focused process expert. I feel reinvigorated in this position. This role allows me to explore my strengths and focus on the things I'm most passionate about, satisfy my need to always be learning and pushing the boundaries of what can be accomplished, and push into a burgeoning field while continuing to make use of and build on my technical expertise. -hunt-

I architect software systems the way I used to handle 911 calls—with precision, urgency, and zero tolerance for failure. As a Software Engineer II at J.B. Hunt, I build mission-critical logistics platforms. My work keeps America's supply chain moving, supporting 10,000+ daily users across 2,000+ terminals with 99.9% uptime. What makes me different? I'm one of the few engineers who's made life-or-death decisions under pressure—first as a Paramedic responding to 2,500+ emergencies, now as a developer building systems that businesses depend on to survive. Recent Wins: 🤖 AI Innovation – Designed and deployed an intelligent support agent that cut ticket resolution time by 50% ⚡ Scale & Reliability – Architected real-time load tracking systems serving 100,000+ platform users with enterprise-grade performance 🏗️ Technical Leadership – Drive frontend architecture decisions and mentor teams on modern development practices My Technical Stack: Expert: JavaScript/TypeScript, Angular 16+, RESTful APIs, Cypress, Vitest Building With: AI/ML Integration, Natural Language Processing Specialized In: High-availability systems, performance optimization, accessibility (WCAG 2.1) Why I Transitioned to Tech: After years of emergency medicine, I realized my real passion was solving complex problems through technology. I completed my Bachlor’s in Computer Science while working full-time, and within 36 months at J.B. Hunt, I advanced from Intern → SE1 → SE2 through consistent delivery and technical leadership. What I Bring to Your Team: ✓ Crisis Management DNA – I stay calm when systems go down and users need answers ✓ Business Impact Focus – I build features that move metrics, not just ship code ✓ AI/ML Expertise – Proven track record deploying production AI systems with measurable ROI ✓ Cross-Functional Leadership – Experience collaborating with product, design, and executive stakeholders Open to opportunities in: Software Engineering, Frontend Architecture, AI/ML Engineering, Technical Leadership roles 📍 Currently based in Searcy, AR | Open to remote opportunities 📧 Best way to reach me: oaklandevans@yahoo.com or DM here Let's connect if you're building: * AI-powered products that solve real business problems * High-scale systems where reliability isn't optional * Teams that value diverse backgrounds and unconventional career paths I respond to most every message, usually within 24 hours. Whether you're hiring, collaborating, or just want to talk about tech—reach out.

results.title: Stuart Grady
highlights:
I earned a BS in Industrial engineering from Mississippi State University. During my time at Mississippi State, I interned with Habitat for Humanity Mississippi Capital Area as well as Ingalls Shipbuilding. I was a crew leader and planner for Habitat for Humanity where I led groups of volunteers, organized build projects, and also help with the physical construction itself. At Ingalls Shipbuilding, I spent my time as an industrial engineering intern. I helped organize ongoing projects by identifying and quantifying pain points and bottlenecks. I also was able to be a part of many time study, utilization, and PFMEA initiatives that helped the shipyard operate more efficiently and smoothly. After I graduated in 2020, I took a logistics engineering role as a part of the Engineering Training and Development Program at J.B. Hunt. For the first 18 months of my time at J.B. Hunt, I spent time on the DCS regional engineering team, the Data Science team, and the Customer Experience team as a part of the ETDP rotational program. While on the DCS Team, I supported the central region of dedicated customers by providing reports and analytics specific to J.B. Hunt customers. I spent my time with the Data Science team as a Product Owner. I was able to guide projects to completion and aid in team structure and organization. I spent my final rotation with the Customer Experience team as a dedicated engineer to a business team to help with their workflows. I was able to analyze the work that was being done and provide detailed plans to update the way work is done on that team to improve employee efficiency and quality of life. In December of 2021, I graduated the rotational program and returned to Data Science to be a product owner full time. From December 2021 to February 2024 I served the DS team owning projects totaling in over 500 million in value. These projects ranged from Pricing models to tracking models to people models. During that time I also spent time as the product owner in charge of all A.I. initiatives at J.B. Hunt, as well as leading another team in addition to Data Science, the Decision Optimization team. In February of 2024 I moved for 10 months to the Decision Optimization full time to help ensure that teams' continued success while they were down ownership and staffing. I returned to own all of Data Science in late 2024, where I currently own all data science projects and work.
...
Since January of 2024 I have been in a Product Owner II role working with two different teams. I spent much of 2024 working exclusively with the Decision Optimization team. I was asked to make this move to help a team that was struggling with a lack of leadership and staffing. This was a rewarding period of my time as a product owner because I was able to help get a team and their work back on track. Some key projects I worked on during this time were Modernized Load Planner as well as SEAGLE. Modernized Load Planner is a rework of an older model that works to improve and optimize the planned routes for drivers to take. SEAGLE is a project that allows users involved in pricing activities to input bids and other pricing info into our model to give optimized recommendations as to the best lanes to take. At the end of 2024, I moved back to the Data Science team full time to own all work happening on that team. I am currently still with Data Science today. Show less
...
During this time I went from owning half of all work that the Data Science team does, to owning all of the work that Data Science does. I oversaw and completed all projects assigned to me. Key projects that I owned and completed are pricing projects worth over $500 Million and the ownership of all A.I. initiatives J.B. Hunt operated at the time.

...

...
Full-time

 Daimler Truck North America is pioneering the enhancement of products and processes through artificial intelligence. As an AI Software Architect for Driver Experience, you'll define and develop AI use cases, document experiences, and showcase AI technologies to improve the Driver Experience department. Responsibilities Define and develop AI use cases with the global Driver Experience development team Document experiences, lessons learned, and solutions for the global team Showcase AI technologies and possibilities to Driver Experience and DTNA teams Research AI trends and evaluate applicability to Driver Experience use cases Organize AI initiative pipeline for the Driver Experience department Skills Bachelors degree in Engineering, Computer Science or similar field and 0-2 years of experience is required Strong fundamentals in software development Ability to communicate technical concepts clearly to both engineers and non-technical stakeholders Ability to build relationships and collaborate with peers Proven ability to rapidly learn and apply new technologies Initiative and ownership of your area of responsibility Comfort with ambiguity and willingness to tackle problems outside your immediate expertise An attached resume is required Experience in automotive or commercial vehicle industry Experience with AI/ML technologies Experience with LLMs, prompt engineering Demonstrated passion for AI evangelism and problem solving through AI Benefits Annual bonus program 401k company contribution with company match up to 6% as well as non-elective company contribution of 3 - 7% depending on age Starting at 4 weeks paid vacation 13+ calendar holidays 8 weeks paid parental leave Employee assistance program Comprehensive healthcare plans and wellness programs Onsite fitness (at some locations) Tuition assistance Volunteer paid time off Short-term and long-term disability plans Company Overview Daimler Truck North America, a Daimler Truck AG company, is the largest heavy-duty truck manufacturer in North America and a leading producer of medium-duty trucks and specialized commercial vehicles. highlights:
# Senior, Data Scientist
...
Sunnyvale, CAHybridSeniorML / AI
...
Senior ML engineer on Walmart's Catalog Data Science team building production GenAI and deep learning models for product attribute extraction, classification, and catalog optimization at petabyte scale.
...
About Team The Catalog Data Science Team at Walmart Global Tech is focused on using the latest research in generative AI (GenAI), artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning (ML), statistics, deep learning, computer vision and optimization to implement solutions that ensure Walmart’s product catalog is accurate, complete, and optimized for customer experience. Our team tackles complex data science and ML engineering challenges related to product classification, attribute extraction, trust & safety, and catalog optimization, empowering next-generation retail use cases. We take pride in writing solid production code in Python, deploying and supporting model services and pipelines, and pushing the boundaries in latency, throughput, and scalability.
...
As a Senior Data Scientist (Machine Learning Engineer) on the Catalog Data Quality team, you will collaborate with other Data Scientists and ML Engineers to develop, deploy, and scale machine learning models in production to extract attributes from product information. You will play a key role in supporting the attribute extraction platform and catalog data quality solutions — adding capabilities and driving adoption of GenAI-powered solutions to increase the coverage and accuracy of the Walmart's product catalog. The Data Science and ML Engineering community at Walmart Global Tech is active in most of the Hack events, utilizing the petabytes of data at our disposal, to build some of the coolest ideas. All the work we do at Walmart Global Tech will eventually benefit our operations & our associates, helping Customers Save Money to Live Better.
...
- Design, develop, and deploy AI/ML, NLP, LLM models into production environments with a focus on reliability and scalability. Own the full model lifecycle — from experimentation and offline evaluation through serving, monitoring, and iterative improvement in production.

...
- Hands-on experience with Generative AI technologies: LLMs, multimodal models, RAG architectures, prompt engineering, and fine-tuning (LoRA/QLoRA)

- Prior Experience in building GenAI, DL, Vision-based models.
...
- Strong foundation in classical ML, deep learning, and modern architectures — CNNs, Transformers, and domain-specific variants

This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for an AI / ML Engineer in the United States.This role sits at the intersection of machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making, focused on solving complex challenges in the energy and utility sector. You will contribute to the development of advanced AI and ML solutions that combine predictive modeling, optimization, and generative AI to support critical infrastructure and sustainability goals. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact. You will work across diverse use cases including forecasting, geospatial analysis, and intelligent decision systems. This is a high-impact role where your work directly supports the modernization and efficiency of energy systems.Accountabilities
...
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and deploy scalable AI and machine learning solutions integrated into production systems.
...
- Build reusable Python libraries and engineering components to support ML pipelines, data processing workflows, and AI system development.
...
- Contribute to the design and implementation of modern AI architectures, including RAG systems, LLM integrations, and agent-based workflows.
...
- Support database design and optimization, including writing efficient queries for large-scale data processing and retrieval.
...
- Translate complex machine learning and AI requirements into structured technical tasks and actionable development plans.
...
- Stay current with advancements in machine learning engineering, generative AI, and AI orchestration, integrating best practices into workflows.
...

...
- 7+ years of experience building, deploying, and maintaining machine learning systems in production environments.
...
- Strong proficiency in Python, including experience building reusable packages and scalable data pipelines.
...
- Hands-on experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP, including CI/CD and containerized deployments.
...
- Solid understanding of ML system design, MLOps principles, and end-to-end model lifecycle management.
...
- Experience with distributed computing frameworks and relational/non-relational databases.
...
- Familiarity with Databricks and large-scale data processing environments.
...
- Experience working with generative AI frameworks such as LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, Hugging Face, or AutoGen.
...
- Exposure to agentic AI systems, AI orchestration, or LLM-based decision workflows is highly desirable.
...
- Nice to have: experience in the utility, energy, or power sector and strong Databricks expertise.

results.title: Distinguished Data Scientist: Associate AI Experience at Walmart | Jeremy
highlights:
Distinguished Data Scientist: Associate AI Experience at Walmart
...
# Distinguished Data Scientist: Associate AI Experience
...
Distinguished individual contributor role leading next-generation agentic AI systems and autonomous agents at enterprise scale across Walmart's global operations, combining deep ML/AI expertise with cross-functional mentorship and high-impact productionized solutions.
...
We are seeking a Distinguished Data Scientist to provide technical leadership and long‑term vision for next‑generation AI systems and platforms. This senior individual contributor will drive breakthrough system design and advanced solution development that transforms Walmart at scale. The role combines deep technical expertise (ML, simulation, optimization, agentic AI) with cross‑functional influence, mentoring of senior talent, and delivery of high‑impact, productionized solutions. You will be a great fit if: - You architect end-to-end ML/AI systems — from feature engineering through production deployment and monitoring - You’ve built and shipped multiple autonomous AI agents (not just experimented with LLM APIs) - You’re Level 5+ on Steve Yegge’s Vibe Coding scale and can ship production systems through AI-assisted development - You combine deep statistical rigor with practical business impact

About the Team The Associate AI Experiences team applies advanced data science, machine learning, and agentic AI to solve high-impact business problems across Walmart globally. You’ll build production Agentic systems, deploy autonomous AI agents, and translate complex data into actionable insights that drive measurable business value at massive scale. Our team builds intelligent platforms, agents and ML-powered solutions that make associates’ jobs easier, faster, and more fulfilling across stores, fulfillment centers, distribution centers, and home office. We leverage the Element LLM Gateway to safely access state-of-the-art language models while keeping all data within Walmart’s network. Our work spans NLP, computer vision, time series forecasting, and multi-agent AI systems at enterprise scale.
